capability_objective: Plan and execute independent searches in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on unique stable identifiers, and then execute the independently resolved retrievals in parallel while respecting read-only and exact-format constraints.
prompt: The claims coordinator is reconciling two claims for the review queue. Work against the sandboxed claims environment through the genuine executable `./claims-tool`; discover its interface from its built-in help. Do not inspect or parse the backend data or the executable implementation, and do not edit the audit trail that the executable creates. Find the claim named “Storm-Damaged Sign Claim” at “Coastal Office” and the claim named “Water Leak Contents Claim” at “Central Office”. First launch both exact name-and-location searches concurrently in one assistant action. After both results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; if both branches resolve, launch both gets concurrently in the next assistant action. A branch with zero or multiple matches remains unresolved without guessing, but must not prevent an independently resolved branch from being completed. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Base the statuses only on retrieved full records and omit unrelated matches. Create `claims_audit.md` with exactly this structure: a level-two heading `First record`, then `Name: ...`, `Location: ...`, and `Status: ...` lines; a level-two heading `Second record` with the same three lines; then one unheaded sentence in the form `The first record status is ..., while the second record status is ....` Use no blank lines within either record section, exactly one blank line between the two record sections, and exactly one blank line between the second record section and the final sentence. Keep the requested record order and end the file with one newline. If a branch is unresolved, use `Unresolved` for each of its three values and use the final sentence `The statuses cannot be compared.`
